text.title: Crash-Pipeline-as-Code Output Contract
text.summary: The methodology produces a `crash-pipeline/` folder in the repo, reviewed and deployed alongside the application. PRs touching crash-tracking that don't update this folder are rejected.

list.item: crash-pipeline/README.md — ownership, runbook link, vendor info, secret references.
crash-pipeline/symbol-manifest.json — per-release record of uploaded symbols + verification result.
symbol-manifest.json entry per release: release_id, platform, dSYM/sourcemap files, upload_status, vendor_url, synthetic_verification_passed_at.
name, condition, threshold, action (channel + on-call), owner, created_at, last_reviewed.

antipattern.description: Symbol upload via developer machine (script lives in /Scripts but is run manually).
antipattern.reason: Violates r1. Drift and forgotten uploads are inevitable.

antipattern.description: Alert rule exists in console with no matching file in crash-pipeline/alert-rules/.
antipattern.reason: Violates r3. Drift-check should fail. Rule cannot survive a vendor migration or admin reset.

antipattern.description: retention.yaml absent OR PII fields set to "send full payload."
Release shipped without symbol-manifest entry OR with verification_passed_at = null.
antipattern.reason: Violates r2. Release likely has un-symbolicated crashes.

list.item: CI runs symbol upload + verification on every release tag.
All alert rules defined in code; drift-check passes daily.
Primary health dashboard is in code with named owner.
Retention + PII settings explicit and reviewed.
Symbol manifest updated per release with verification timestamp.
